common.noResultsBody: We're inviting the best venues in {cityFrom}. In the meantime, browse other neighborhoods or nominate your favorite studio.

yoga.warszawa: Yoga in Warsaw has its rhythm — morning vinyasa in Mokotów, candlelit yin in Praga at dusk. Hakuna gathers studios, teachers, and open classes worth checking today. Filter by neighborhood, time, or level, book online, and show up on the mat. Whether it's your first beginner class or a serious ashtanga practice, you'll find a fit here.

items.question: How much do yoga classes in Warsaw cost?
items.answer: A drop-in is usually 50–80 PLN, monthly unlimited 200–360 PLN depending on studio and neighborhood. Many places offer a free trial week or a 1 PLN intro class — check the studio page.

items.question: Can I start yoga with zero experience?
items.answer: Yes — classes labeled 'beginner' or 'basic' are exactly for that. Mats are usually provided. Wear something comfortable and breathable, bring water.

items.question: Which yoga style should I start with?
items.answer: Hatha and vinyasa are the most beginner-friendly. Hatha is slower with more time on alignment. Vinyasa is more dynamic and links movement to breath. Yin and restorative are great evening sessions for unwinding.

items.question: How often should I practice?
items.answer: 2–3 sessions a week is plenty for visible benefits. Most studios offer monthly passes that pay off from 6 visits a month.

items.question: Can I book a single class or do I need a pass?
items.answer: Yes — Hakuna lets you book single classes online with no commitment. Buy a pass once you've found a studio and style that fits.

cityHooks.warszawa: The capital blends business-district energy, alt scenes on the right bank, and quieter green areas like Mokotów or Żoliborz — every neighborhood has its rhythm.
cityHooks.krakow: From artsy Kazimierz to athletic Krowodrza — Kraków offers a fit for every lifestyle.
cityHooks.wroclaw: Student energy in Śródmieście, café vibes in Krzyki, industrial pull in Fabryczna — Wrocław pulls you in with variety.
cityHooks.gdansk: Baltic mood in Wrzeszcz and Oliwa meets the buzz of Śródmieście — Gdańsk is a city that pairs movement with nature.
cityHooks.poznan: Poznań packs a dense, walkable urban fabric — from the historic Old Town to creative Jeżyce. Activities are always close by.

meta.title: About Hakuna — local activity discovery platform
meta.description: Hakuna helps you find and book activities near you. Learn about our vision, team, and story.

About.heroBody: Hakuna is a platform for people who'd rather discover than plan. We connect you with local studios, workshops and open sessions — the kind of tiny gatherings that make a neighborhood feel like home.

spontaneity.title: Spontaneity first
spontaneity.text: The best moments don't fit in a spreadsheet. We surface what's starting in the next two hours.

local.title: Local by design
local.text: Every host, studio and instructor on Hakuna is rooted in your city. No franchises, no fluff.

curated.title: Curated, not catalogued
curated.text: We hand-pick experiences that feel alive — not just the ones with the biggest marketing budgets.

y2023.label: The idea
y2023.text: Hakuna starts as a weekend experiment: how do we help people say yes to their city?

y2024.label: First cities
y2024.text: Private beta with 450+ local hosts across Warsaw, Berlin and London.

y2026.label: Today
y2026.text: Opening the doors to everyone — with a public beta and a fast-growing community.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy describes how Hakuna collects, uses, and protects information about you when you use our platform. By using Hakuna, you agree to the practices described below.

collect.heading: Information we collect
body: We collect the information you give us directly — when you create an account, book an activity, or contact us. That includes your name, email, and payment details.
We also automatically collect technical information such as device type, browser, IP address, and pages viewed, so we can keep the platform secure and improve performance.

use.heading: How we use your information
body: We use what we collect to provide and improve the service, process bookings, send confirmations and the occasional product update, and detect fraud.
We don't sell your personal information. We share data with service providers only to the extent necessary to run the platform — for example, payment processors.

cookies.heading: Cookies and tracking
body: Hakuna uses cookies and similar technologies to remember your preferences, keep you signed in, and understand how the platform is used. You can manage cookie preferences from your browser at any time.

retention.heading: Data retention
body: We retain your personal information for as long as your account is active, or as needed to provide the service and comply with legal obligations. You can request deletion at any time by contacting us.

rights.heading: Your rights
body: Depending on your location, you may have the right to access, correct, port, or delete your personal data. To use any of these rights, reach out via the contact channels on this site.

contact.heading: Contact us
body: Questions about this Privacy Policy or how we handle your data? Email privacy@hakuna.example.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms of Service govern your access to and use of the Hakuna platform. By creating an account or booking an activity, you agree to them.

eligibility.heading: Eligibility
body: You must be at least 18 years old to create a Hakuna account. If you book on behalf of a minor, you're responsible for meeting the venue's requirements.

bookings.heading: Bookings and payments
body: When you book an activity, you enter into an agreement directly with the host or studio. Hakuna acts as a marketplace and processes payments on the host's behalf.
Fees are shown at checkout and include applicable taxes. Some activities require non-refundable deposits — this is clearly marked on the listing.

cancellation.heading: Cancellations
body: Unless the listing says otherwise, you can cancel free of charge up to 48 hours before the activity. After that, refunds are at the host's discretion.

conduct.heading: User conduct
body: You agree not to use Hakuna to harass other users, post misleading content, or try to bypass platform security. Accounts that break these rules may be suspended without notice.

liability.heading: Liability
body: Hakuna is not liable for injuries, damages, or losses arising from your participation in an activity. Always follow the host and venue instructions, and consult a professional if you have medical concerns.

changes.heading: Changes to these terms
body: We may update these Terms from time to time. Material changes are communicated via the platform or email at least 14 days before taking effect.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.intro: This Cookie Policy explains what cookies are, how Hakuna uses them, and how to manage your preferences.

what.heading: What are cookies?
body: Cookies are small text files placed on your device when you visit a website. They help sites remember information about your visit, such as your preferred language and sign-in state.

types.heading: Types of cookies we use
body: Essential cookies keep the platform running — for example, remembering your session between pages.
Performance cookies help us understand how the platform is used so we can improve it. These are anonymous and aggregated.
Preference cookies remember your settings (language, city, favorites) to personalize your experience.

third.heading: Third-party cookies
body: Some features rely on trusted third parties such as payment processors and analytics providers. Those providers may set their own cookies under their own privacy policies.

managing.heading: Managing cookies
body: Most browsers let you refuse or delete cookies from the settings menu. Blocking essential cookies may stop parts of the platform from working as expected.

contact.heading: Contact
body: Questions about cookies? Email privacy@hakuna.example and we'll help.

sample.title: Urban Flow: The Elite Performance Workshop
sample.description: Push your boundaries in this high-intensity masterclass designed for performers who want to master the fusion of contemporary movement and urban precision. Led by internationally acclaimed choreographer Marcus Thorne, the session focuses on spatial awareness, explosive power, and emotional storytelling.

sample.name: Urban Rhythm Academy
sample.about: Urban Rhythm Academy is a hub for dancers, performers and movement-lovers who want more than a class — they want a community. Our instructors are working professionals, our studios are built for serious training, and our philosophy is simple: movement should feel as good as it looks.

a1.title: Sunrise Vinyasa Flow
a1.description: Wake the body up with a breath-led flow by the rooftop garden. All levels welcome — mats provided.

a2.title: Intro to Wheel Throwing
a2.description: Shape your first bowl on a spinning wheel. Small group, hands-on guidance, clay and firing included.

a3.title: Open Padel Session
a3.description: Drop in on a friendly mixed-level padel session. Rackets available, beginners rotate every game.

a4.title: Urban Run Club
a4.description: Easy 5K loop through the park with pace groups and a coffee stop after. Bring water.

a5.title: Urban Flow Masterclass
a5.description: High-energy contemporary class blending hip-hop and flow. Mirrors, sprung floor, pro sound system.

a6.title: Advanced Clay Tennis
a6.description: Technique-focused match play on red clay. For intermediate/advanced players working on their slide.

a7.title: Bouldering Basics
a7.description: First time climbing? Learn to read routes, fall safe, and send your first V0s with a friendly coach.

a8.title: Sunset Boxing
a8.description: Shake off the day with pad rounds, footwork drills, and a full-body circuit. Gloves on site.

a9.title: Pasta From Scratch
a9.description: Roll fresh tagliatelle and ravioli, then sit down together over wine. Ingredients and apron included.

a10.title: Watercolor Basics
a10.description: Loose, expressive watercolor techniques for total beginners. Paper, brushes, and paints provided.

s1.title: Sunset Hatha Flow
s1.description: Slow, grounded Hatha sequence lit by golden-hour light. Perfect for unwinding and improving flexibility.

s2.title: Advanced Clay Tennis
s2.description: Drill-heavy session on red clay focused on footwork, sliding, and match tactics. Bring two balls.

s3.title: Morning Lap Swim
s3.description: Structured lane-based swim with set intervals for all paces. Heated 25m pool, showers and towels included.

s4.title: Open Mic Guitar Night
s4.description: Bring your guitar and a song or two. Warm crowd, pro PA, a full bar, and sign-ups on the door.

r1.name: Anna Kowalska
r1.text: Hakuna changed my weekends. I found a local pottery workshop 200 metres from my flat — one I had no idea about. Booking took 20 seconds.

r2.name: Marek Nowak
r2.text: Spontaneous padel matches in the neighbourhood were a game-changer. On Hakuna it's all live — I see what's starting in an hour and I just go.

r3.name: Sophia Lindqvist
r3.text: Love how curated the experiences feel. Every instructor I've booked has been top-tier. The app finally respects my time.

r4.name: Daniel Osei
r4.text: The 'Closest to You' feature is pure magic — I booked a sunrise run with strangers that became my Tuesday ritual.

r5.name: Léa Moreau
r5.text: Clean design, honest pricing, real communities. The city feels a little more alive.

r6.name: Tomáš Dvořák
r6.text: Great for travelling. I dropped into a boxing class in Berlin the same night I landed.

blog.title: The Hakuna Journal
blog.subtitle: Stories, guides and city picks from the people who make your weekends worth showing up for.

best-dance-schools-warsaw.title: The best dance schools in Warsaw — 2026 ranking
best-dance-schools-warsaw.subtitle: Five studios actually worth signing up for — from bachata to contemporary.
best-dance-schools-warsaw.excerpt: We checked dozens of Warsaw dance schools. Here are the five that beginners and advanced dancers both rate consistently.
best-dance-schools-warsaw.heroAlt: Dance class in a studio at dusk

body.text: A good dance school isn't the most expensive floor or the loudest Instagram. It's a schedule that fits your week, a teacher who remembers your name, and a room full of people at your level.
We pulled the five Warsaw studios that consistently get the highest ratings from Hakuna students — across styles, neighborhoods, and budgets.
The ranking

items.name: Studio Rytm — Śródmieście
items.imageAlt: Dance studio with mirrors and wooden floor
items.summary: The flagship latin studio downtown — classes running until 22:00, groups from zero to advanced, and monthly open practicas anyone can drop into.
pros: More levels under one roof than anywhere else in the city
Teachers lead in pairs — easier to pick up lead/follow
Passes valid for 60 days instead of the usual 30

items.name: Kolektyw Współczesny — Praga
items.summary: A post-industrial room in a former print shop, a small crew, and a vibe that's more art studio than fitness class. Taught by dancers from Teatr Wielki.
pros: Great for people coming back after a break
Regular weekend workshops with visiting guests
cons: Group classes only — no private sessions

items.name: Hip-Hop Lab — Wola
items.summary: A big space with the best energy in town. Weekly open classes, monthly battles, and a teacher pipeline — a home for people who want to dance a lot.
pros: Best price of any large studio in the city
Strong beginner scene for kids and teens
cons: Classes can get very large (25+ people)

items.name: Ballet Room — Mokotów
items.summary: A classic studio tucked inside a tenement — small room, big discipline, conservatory-trained teachers. Good for adults returning to ballet.
pros: Very technical, careful work on posture

items.name: Swing Warsaw — Żoliborz
items.imageAlt: Couple dancing swing on a wooden floor
items.summary: The oldest swing scene in the city. Semester-based courses and weekly Friday socials — the easiest way into a tight but warm community.

body.text: How to pick one for yourself

Start with the style you keep going back to on YouTube. Then filter by neighborhood and door-to-door time — 15 minutes of commute is the difference between habit and maybe next week. Finally, book a single drop-in before you commit to a pass.
The best dance school is the one you actually leave home for on a rainy Tuesday.

warsaw-weekend-guide.title: A perfect Warsaw weekend, minute by minute
warsaw-weekend-guide.subtitle: From Powiśle breakfasts to Praga basslines — how locals actually spend their Saturdays.
warsaw-weekend-guide.excerpt: A living, walkable 48-hour itinerary through Warsaw: the bakeries, the parks, and the tiny studios worth crossing the river for.

body.text: Warsaw doesn't hand itself over on a map. The best parts of the weekend are the small choices — the bakery over the chain café, the outdoor yoga class over another museum queue, the walk along the river instead of the taxi across it.
Saturday morning: slow start in Powiśle

Start at a bakery on Browarna, grab a cinnamon bun and a flat white, and wander down to the riverbank. By 10:00 the pavement is full of runners, skateboarders and the occasional sunrise yoga group rolling up their mats.

body.alt: Vistula riverbank on a sunny morning
body.caption: The Vistula boulevards are the city's living room on a warm weekend.

body.text: Afternoon: cross the river
Walk the Świętokrzyski bridge into Praga. The neighborhood is loud, green and unfinished — which is exactly why the best new studios, workshops and record stores keep opening here.
Praga is the only part of Warsaw that still surprises me every weekend.
Sunday: one class, one long lunch
Book one thing on Hakuna — a pottery session, a paddle match, a sunrise swim — then leave the rest of the day open. Sunday's secret is not doing too much.

morning-yoga-habits.title: How five minutes of morning yoga changes your week
morning-yoga-habits.subtitle: You don't need a 90-minute flow. You need a ritual you'll actually keep.
morning-yoga-habits.excerpt: A tiny morning practice — three poses, five minutes, no mat required — beats the perfect class you skip every Tuesday.
morning-yoga-habits.heroAlt: Sunrise yoga on a wooden floor

body.text: The perfect class doesn't count if it's on your calendar and not in your body. The practice that changes your week is the one small enough to happen on a Tuesday when you're tired.
The five-minute rule
Three poses. Five breaths each. That's it. Cat-cow to wake the spine, a low lunge to open the hips, and a seated twist before coffee. You're done before the kettle boils.
You don't rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your habits.
When to add the full class
Once the five-minute version has held for two full weeks, book a studio class once a week. The ritual carries you through the days you don't feel like going — which, let's be honest, is most of them.

body.alt: Yoga mats rolled up in a sunlit studio
body.caption: Studios near you on Hakuna — pick one and book for Saturday morning.

family-friendly-weekends.title: Family-friendly weekends that don't cost a fortune
family-friendly-weekends.subtitle: Six low-effort, high-joy ideas for parents who want a real weekend too.
family-friendly-weekends.excerpt: You don't need a theme park. You need a short list of nearby things you can turn into a Saturday — and a backup for when it rains.
family-friendly-weekends.heroAlt: Parents and kids playing in a park

body.text: The trick to a good family weekend is the same as any weekend: less ambition, more proximity. A 15-minute walk is a lower bar than a 45-minute drive, and you'll do the walk twice as often.
One booking, one backup
Book one thing ahead — a parent-and-kid pottery class, a junior bouldering session, a Saturday swim — and hold one backup in your back pocket for rain. Everything else, improvise.

body.alt: Kids painting at a weekend workshop
body.caption: Hands-on classes beat screen-based ones every time.

body.text: Keep the same Saturday
Kids love repetition more than variety. A "Saturday routine" — same park, same café, same class — is cheaper, calmer and quietly becomes the part of the week everyone looks forward to.

why-run-clubs-work.title: Why run clubs work (and how to find one that fits)
why-run-clubs-work.subtitle: The science is clear and the vibes are unserious. A short field guide to joining your first group run.
why-run-clubs-work.excerpt: Solo runs are a habit. Group runs are a social life. Here's how to find a club that matches your pace — and your Tuesday evenings.
why-run-clubs-work.heroAlt: Group run through a city park at dawn

body.text: A run club is three things at once: a weekly workout, a low-pressure friend group, and a reason to leave the apartment when you really don't want to. That's a good deal for zero cost.
Pick by pace, not by proximity
A club five minutes away at the wrong pace is worse than one 20 minutes away at yours. Ask about the slowest group, not the fastest — good clubs lead with their beginner pace.
Nobody got into running to race. We got into running to talk.
body.cite: Founder of a Warsaw run crew

body.text: Show up three times
The first run is a blind date. The second is a second opinion. The third is when you know. Give every new club three Tuesdays before you decide.

pottery-beginners-guide.title: Your first pottery class: what to expect
pottery-beginners-guide.subtitle: The tools, the mess, and why your first bowl will be perfectly imperfect.
pottery-beginners-guide.excerpt: Two hours, clay under your nails, and a slightly wonky bowl you'll keep forever. Here's what actually happens in a beginner pottery class.
pottery-beginners-guide.coverAlt: Hands shaping clay on a potter's wheel

body.text: Nobody walks into their first pottery class and makes a nice bowl. That's the point. You're not there for the object — you're there for two hours where the only task is your hands, a spinning wheel, and the wet clay in front of you.
What to bring
Nothing. The studio has aprons, clay, tools and water. Wear something you don't mind getting dirty, tie your hair back, and take your rings off before you sit down.

body.alt: Finished pottery pieces drying on a shelf
body.caption: Your first piece goes on the shelf to dry — you'll pick it up a week later.

body.text: The quiet part
Most people leave pottery class saying the same thing: they didn't think about their phone, their job or their week for the whole two hours. That's the real reason to book it.

meta.title: Become a Hakuna partner
meta.description: Apply to list your studio, school, or club on Hakuna's activity marketplace in Poland.

PartnerApply.title: Become a Hakuna partner
PartnerApply.subtitle: Fill in this short form — we'll review your application and get back to you within 3 business days.
PartnerApply.replacementNote: This replaces the old 'Host an activity' signup form.

error.rateLimited: Too many attempts. Try again in {minutes, plural, one {# minute} other {# minutes}}.
error.botCheck: We couldn't confirm you're not a bot. Refresh the page and try again.
error.server: Something went wrong on our side. Please try again in a moment.

success.heading: We've got your application
success.body: A confirmation has been sent to the email you provided. We'll be in touch within 3 business days.

Consent.title: We use cookies
Consent.description: We use cookies to make Hakuna work, measure traffic, and serve you better. You choose what's on.

essential.title: Essential
essential.description: Required for the site to work — sign-in, bookings, security. These can't be turned off.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.description: Anonymous pageview stats on class listings. They help us improve Hakuna.

bookings.title: Your bookings
bookings.subtitle: Upcoming and past sessions in one place.

favorites.title: Favorites
favorites.subtitle: {count, plural, =0 {No saved activities yet} one {# saved activity} other {# saved activities}}
favorites.empty: Nothing saved yet. Tap the heart on an activity to bring it here.

calendar.title: Google Calendar
calendar.subtitle: Connect Google Calendar — your bookings show up there automatically.
calendar.about: We create an event in your calendar after a booking is confirmed and remove it on cancellation. Only events we created.
calendar.confirmDisconnect: Disconnect calendar? Existing events stay in your Google Calendar.

errors.missing_config: Google integration isn't configured on our side.
errors.not_signed_in: Sign in to connect your calendar.

export.heading: Download your data
export.description: Generate a JSON file with everything we hold about you — profile, bookings, reviews.

delete.heading: Delete account
delete.description: Request account deletion. Within 30 days your personal data will be anonymized and the account removed. Bookings are kept for financial records.
delete.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete your account?
delete.pending: Deletion requested — your data will be removed on {date}.

BookingDetail.title: Your booking
BookingDetail.checkoutSuccessBody: Thanks! Confirmation will appear here within a few seconds — refresh if you don't see it yet.
BookingDetail.checkoutCancelledBody: We didn't charge you. You can try again from the activity page.
BookingDetail.cancelConfirmBody: You'll receive a full refund. This cannot be undone.
BookingDetail.cancelSuccess: Cancelled. Your refund will arrive in 1–2 business days.

cancelError.too_late: Bookings within 48 hours can't be cancelled online — contact the venue.
